lt.gd: This bill supplements or reduces appropriations otherwise provided for the support and operation of state government for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2025 and ending June 30, 2026 and appropriates funds for the support and operation of state government for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2026 and ending June 30, 2027.
lt.hp: This bill:provides appropriations for the use and support of certain state agencies;authorizes rates for certain internal service funds;provides appropriations for other purposes as described; andprovides intent language.
lt.moni: Money Appropriated in this Bill:This bill appropriates $27,802,400 in operating and capital budgets for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $1,784,500 in expendable funds and accounts for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates ($963,200) in business-like activities for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $1,500,000 in restricted fund and account transfers for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates ($100) in fiduciary funds for fiscal year 2026, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $1,354,206,600 in operating and capital budgets for fiscal year 2027, including:$948,956,900 from General Fund; and$288,000 from Income Tax Fund; and$404,961,700 from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $25,381,000 in expendable funds and accounts for fiscal year 2027, including:$3,775,900 from General Fund; and$21,605,100 from various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $87,204,400 in business-like activities for fiscal year 2027, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.This bill appropriates $25,968,400 in restricted fund and account transfers for fiscal year 2027, all of which is from the General Fund.This bill appropriates $1,313,000 in fiduciary funds for fiscal year 2027, all of which is from the various sources as detailed in this bill.
lt.oc: Other Special Clauses:This bill provides a special effective date.

leg.enact: Be it enacted by the Legislature of the state of Utah:

sectionText.summary: The following sums of money are appropriated for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2025, and ending June 30, 2026. These are additions to amounts previously appropriated for fiscal year 2026.

licat.summary: Under the terms and conditions of Title 63J, Chapter 1, Budgetary Procedures Act, the Legislature appropriates the following sums of money from the funds or accounts indicated for the use and support of the government of the state of Utah.

lineitem.name: CCJJ - Factual Innocence Payments
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - CCJJ - Factual Innocence Payments

lineitem.name: CCJJ - Jail Reimbursement
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - CCJJ - Jail Reimbursement

lineitem.name: Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice

lineitem.name: Indigent Defense Commission
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Indigent Defense Commission

lineitem.name: Attorney General
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Attorney General
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $5,000,000 provided for the Office of the Attorney General - Attorney General in Item 41, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $5,000,000 for accelerating casework.

lineitem.name: Children's Justice Centers
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Children's Justice Centers
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $1,000,000 provided for the Office of the Attorney General - Children's Justice Centers in Item 42, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $1,000,000 for Local Children's Justice Centers.

lineitem.name: Contract Attorneys
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Contract Attorneys
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $2,000,000 provided for the Office of the Attorney General - Contract Attorneys in Item 43, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $2,000,000 for civil litigation.

lineitem.name: Prosecution Council
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Prosecution Council
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $300,000 provided for the Office of the Attorney General - Prosecution Council in Item 44, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $300,000 for prosecutor training and technical assistance.

lineitem.name: Board of Pardons and Parole Operations
lineitem.lidesc: Board of Pardons and Parole - Board of Pardons and Parole Operations

lineitem.name: Administration
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Administration

program.name: Prison Operations Utah State Correctional Facility
program.display: Prison Operations Utah State Correctional Facility

lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $500,000 provided for the Utah Department of Corrections - Administration in Item 46, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $500,000 for equipment, inmate support, training, and capital improvement.

lineitem.name: Jail Contracting
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Jail Contracting
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $5,000,000 provided for the Utah Department of Corrections - Jail Contracting in Item 47, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $5,000,000 for housing inmates, treatment, and vocational programs at county jails.

lineitem.name: County Correctional Facility Contracting Reserve
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - County Correctional Facility Contracting Reserve
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $2,500,000 provided for the Utah Department of Corrections - County Correctional Facility Contracting Reserve not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $2,500,000 for housing inmates and treatment at county jails.

lineitem.name: Adult Probation and Parole
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Adult Probation and Parole
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $2,500,000 provided for the Utah Department of Corrections - Adult Probation and Parole in Item 49, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $2,500,000 for equipment, inmate support, training, and capital improvement.

lineitem.name: Prison Operations
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Prison Operations
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $3,000,000 provided for the Utah Department of Corrections - Prison Operations in Item 50, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $3,000,000 for equipment, inmate support, training, and capital improvement.

lineitem.name: Re-Entry and Rehabilitation
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Re-Entry and Rehabilitation
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $4,000,000 provided for the Utah Department of Corrections - Re-Entry and Rehabilitation in Item 51, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $4,000,000 for equipment, inmate support, capital improvement, and officer recruitment.

lineitem.name: Administration
lineitem.lidesc: Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Administration
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $3,225,000 provided for the Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Administration in Item 59, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $3,200,000 for for Judicial Council allocated one-time expenses including but not limited to IT projects, employee recruitment and retention, and Utah code purchases; and up to $25,000 for Wellness Council carryforward.

lineitem.name: Contracts and Leases
lineitem.lidesc: Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Contracts and Leases
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $500,000 provided for the Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Contracts and Leases in Item 53, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $500,000 for one-time facility related expenses.

lineitem.name: Guardian ad Litem
lineitem.lidesc: Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Guardian ad Litem
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $500,000 provided for the Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Guardian ad Litem in Item 55, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $500,000 for GAL Special Projects, including potential incentives for employees.

lineitem.name: Jury and Witness Fees
lineitem.lidesc: Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Jury and Witness Fees
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $2,000,000 provided for the Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Jury and Witness Fees in Item 56, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $2,000,000 for Interpretation services.

lineitem.name: Division of Homeland Security - Emergency and Disaster Management
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Division of Homeland Security - Emergency and Disaster Management

lineitem.name: Driver License
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Driver License
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $1,000,000 provided for the Department of Public Safety - Driver License in Item 78, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $1,000,000 for reserve for Uninsured Motorist Database.

lineitem.name: Emergency Management
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Emergency Management
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $2,500,000 provided for the Department of Public Safety - Emergency Management in Item 79, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $600,000 for carryover of Local Disaster Funding; up to $1,000,000 for reserve for purchase of emergency equipment supplies, etc. in case of emergency; and up to $900,000 for technology upgrades and computer replacements.

lineitem.name: Highway Safety
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Highway Safety
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $400,000 provided for the Department of Public Safety - Highway Safety in Item 81, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $400,000 for technology replacement purchases, upgrades to grant management system, and one-time expenditures not allowed by grant funds..

lineitem.name: Peace Officers' Standards and Training
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Peace Officers' Standards and Training

appsrc.name: Uninsured Motorist Identification Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From Uninsured Motorist Identification Restricted Account, One-time

lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $500,000 provided for the Department of Public Safety - Peace Officers' Standards and Training in Item 58, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $400,000 for repairs to gun range and other training facilities; and up to $100,000 for replacement of laptops, printers, other equipment.

lineitem.name: Programs & Operations
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Programs Operations

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Electronic Cigarette Substance and Nicotine Product Proceeds Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Electronic Cigarette Substance and Nicotine Product Proceeds Restricted Account, One-time

lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $12,000,000 provided for the Department of Public Safety - Programs Operations in Item 59, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $2,500,000 for Early Intervention Grant Carryover; up to $2,000,000 for fair park and downtown cleanup; up to $1,000,000 for HUB project/other technology; up to $1,000,000 for Line of duty death scholarships carryover, correctional facility grant carryover, license plate program carryover; up to $2,000,000 for Mental Health Grant Carryover; and up to $3,500,000 for trooper laptops, body armor, cameras, and other equipment etc.

lineitem.name: Bureau of Criminal Identification
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Bureau of Criminal Identification
lineitem.appintent: Under terms of Section 63J-1-603 Utah Code Annotated, the Legislature intends that up to $3,000,000 provided for the Department of Public Safety - Bureau of Criminal Identification in Item 60, Chapter 7, Laws of Utah 2025 not lapse at the close of fiscal year 2026. Use of any nonlapsing funds is limited to: up to $500,000 for overtime and other operating costs; up to $1,000,000 for replacement of laptops, printers, other equipment for licensing; and up to $1,500,000 for UCJIS and other BCI system upgrades.

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed the following expendable funds. The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer amounts between funds and accounts as indicated. Outlays and expenditures from the funds or accounts to which the money is transferred may be made without further legislative action, in accordance with statutory provisions relating to the funds or accounts.

lineitem.name: Crime Victim Reparations Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Crime Victim Reparations Fund

lineitem.name: Justice Assistance Grant Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Justice Assistance Grant Fund

lineitem.name: IDC - Child Welfare Parental Defense Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - IDC - Child Welfare Parental Defense Fund

lineitem.name: Pretrial Release Programs Special Revenue Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Pretrial Release Programs Special Revenue Fund

lineitem.name: Crime and Violence Prevention Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Crime and Violence Prevention Fund

lineitem.name: Attorney General Litigation Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Attorney General Litigation Fund

lineitem.name: Alcoholic Beverage Control Act Enforcement Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Alcoholic Beverage Control Act Enforcement Fund

program.name: Alcoholic Beverage Control Act Enforcement Fund
program.display: Alcoholic Beverage Control Act Enforcement Fund

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed the following proprietary funds. Under the terms and conditions of Utah Code 63J-1-410, for any included Internal Service Fund, the Legislature approves budgets, full-time permanent positions, and capital acquisition amounts as indicated, and appropriates to the funds, as indicated, estimated revenue from rates, fees, and other charges. The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer amounts between funds and accounts as indicated.

lineitem.name: ISF - Attorney General
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - ISF - Attorney General
lineitem.appintent: Under the terms and conditions of Utah Code Title 63J Chapter 1 and other fee statutes as applicable, the following fees and rates are approved for the use and support of the government of the State of Utah for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2025, and ending June 30, 2026. These fees override and replace those for the Attorney General internal service fund contained in Laws of Utah 2025, Chapter 169. Attorney - Co-located Rate (per Hour) = $163.00 Attorney - Office Rate (per Hour) = $168.00Investigator - Co-located Rate = Actual costInvestigator - Office Rate = Actual costParalegal - Co-located Rate (per Hour) = $76.00Paralegal - Office Rate (per Hour) = $79.00

lineitem.name: Utah Correctional Industries
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Utah Correctional Industries

lineitem.name: Local Government Emergency Response Loan Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Local Government Emergency Response Loan Fund

licat.summary: The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer the following amounts between the following funds or accounts as indicated. Expenditures and outlays from the funds to which the money is transferred must be authorized by an appropriation.

lineitem.name: General Fund Restricted - Employment Incentive Restricted Account
lineitem.lidesc: General Fund Restricted - Employment Incentive Restricted Account

lineitem.name: General Fund Restricted - Emergency Medical Services System Account
lineitem.lidesc: General Fund Restricted - Emergency Medical Services System Account

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed proposed revenues, expenditures, fund balances, and changes in fund balances for the following fiduciary funds.

lineitem.name: Indigent Inmate Trust Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Indigent Inmate Trust Fund

lineitem.name: Financial Crimes Trust Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Financial Crimes Trust Fund

sectionText.summary: The following sums of money are appropriated for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2026, and ending June 30, 2027. These are additions to amounts previously appropriated for fiscal year 2027.

licat.summary: Under the terms and conditions of Title 63J, Chapter 1, Budgetary Procedures Act, the Legislature appropriates the following sums of money from the funds or accounts indicated for the use and support of the government of the state of Utah.

lineitem.name: CCJJ - Factual Innocence Payments
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - CCJJ - Factual Innocence Payments

lineitem.name: CCJJ - Jail Reimbursement
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - CCJJ - Jail Reimbursement

lineitem.name: Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Victim Services Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Victim Services Restricted Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Criminal Forfeiture Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Criminal Forfeiture Restricted Account

program.name: Utah Office for Victims of Crime
program.display: Utah Office for Victims of Crime

lineitem.name: Indigent Defense Commission
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Indigent Defense Commission

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Indigent Defense Resources
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Indigent Defense Resources

lineitem.name: Attorney General
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Attorney General

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Consumer Privacy Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Consumer Privacy Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Tobacco Settlement Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Tobacco Settlement Account

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Office of the Attorney General report on the following Attorney General line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Attorney and Staff Assesment (Target = 90) and 2. Bills Modified After SWAP Input (Target = 70%).

lineitem.name: Children's Justice Centers
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Children's Justice Centers

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Victim Services Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Victim Services Restricted Account

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Office of the Attorney General report on the following Children's Justice Centers line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Caregiver References (Target = 90.9%); 2. Caregiver Satisfaction (Target = 93.0%); and 3. Multidisciplinary Teams (Target = 89.1%).

lineitem.name: Contract Attorneys
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Contract Attorneys

lineitem.name: Prosecution Council
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Prosecution Council
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Office of the Attorney General report on the following Prosecution Council line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Trauma-informed Training (Target = 87%); 2. Trial Without Domestic Violence Victim (Target = 80%); and 3. Utah Prosecution Council Conferences (Target = 60%).

lineitem.name: Board of Pardons and Parole Operations
lineitem.lidesc: Board of Pardons and Parole - Board of Pardons and Parole Operations
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Board of Pardons and Parole report on the following Board of Pardons and Parole Operations line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Measure of Alignment of Board Decisions with the Guidelines (Target = 60%); 2. Measure of Parole Revocations (Target = 0.3); 3. Measure of Recidivism (Target = 70%); and 4. Measure of Time Under Board Jurisdiction (Target = 5).

lineitem.name: Administration
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Administration
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Utah Department of Corrections report on the following Administration line item performance measure for FY 2027: Supervision Early Termination (Target = 30%).

lineitem.name: Jail Contracting
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Jail Contracting
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Utah Department of Corrections report on the following Jail Contracting line item performance measure for FY 2027: Programming in Jail Contracting (Target = 60%).

lineitem.name: County Correctional Facility Contracting Reserve
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - County Correctional Facility Contracting Reserve

lineitem.name: Adult Probation and Parole
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Adult Probation and Parole

appsrc.display: From G.F.R. - Interstate Compact for Adult Offender Supervision

lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Prison Operations

lineitem.name: Re-Entry and Rehabilitation
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Re-Entry and Rehabilitation

appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Opioid Litigation Proceeds Restricted Account

appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Prison Telephone Surcharge Account

lineitem.name: Division of Homeland Security - Emergency and Disaster Management
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Division of Homeland Security - Emergency and Disaster Management
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Public Safety report on the following Division of Homeland Security - Emergency and Disaster Management line item performance measure for FY 2027: DEM Distribution of Funds (Target = 100%).

lineitem.name: Driver License
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Driver License

appsrc.name: Department of Public Safety Restricted Account

appsrc.display: From Public Safety Motorcycle Education Fund

appsrc.display: From Uninsured Motorist Identification Restricted Account

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Public Safety report on the following Driver License line item performance measure for FY 2027: Average Customer Call Wait Time (seconds) (Target = 30).

lineitem.name: Emergency Management
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Emergency Management

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - State Disaster Recovery Restr Acct
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - State Disaster Recovery Restr Acct

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Public Safety report on the following Emergency Management line item performance measure for FY 2027: Percentage of personnel that have completed the required National Incident Management System training (Target = 100%).

lineitem.name: Emergency Management - National Guard Response
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Emergency Management - National Guard Response

lineitem.name: Highway Safety
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Highway Safety

appsrc.name: Department of Public Safety Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From Department of Public Safety Restricted Account

appsrc.display: From Public Safety Motorcycle Education Fund

lineitem.name: Peace Officers' Standards and Training
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Peace Officers' Standards and Training

appsrc.name: Uninsured Motorist Identification Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From Uninsured Motorist Identification Restricted Account

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Public Safety report on the following Peace Officers' Standards and Training line item performance measures for FY 2027: 1. Officer Training Completion (Target = 100%) and 2. Percentage of Presented Cases of Law Enforcement Personnel (Target = 95%).

lineitem.name: Programs & Operations
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Programs Operations

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Public Safety report on the following Programs Operations line item performance measure for FY 2027: Median DNA Case Turnaround Time (Target = 60).

lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Bureau of Criminal Identification

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Concealed Weapons Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Concealed Weapons Account

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Department of Public Safety report on the following Bureau of Criminal Identification line item performance measure for FY 2027: Percentage of LiveScan fingerprint card data entered into the Utah Computerized Criminal History (UCCH) and Automated fingerprint identification System (AFIS) databases, or deleted from the queue in 7 days (Target = 100%).

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed the following expendable funds. The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer amounts between funds and accounts as indicated. Outlays and expenditures from the funds or accounts to which the money is transferred may be made without further legislative action, in accordance with statutory provisions relating to the funds or accounts.

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed the following proprietary funds. Under the terms and conditions of Utah Code 63J-1-410, for any included Internal Service Fund, the Legislature approves budgets, full-time permanent positions, and capital acquisition amounts as indicated, and appropriates to the funds, as indicated, estimated revenue from rates, fees, and other charges. The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer amounts between funds and accounts as indicated.

lineitem.name: ISF - Attorney General
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - ISF - Attorney General

lineitem.name: Utah Correctional Industries
lineitem.lidesc: Utah Department of Corrections - Utah Correctional Industries

lineitem.name: Local Government Emergency Response Loan Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Department of Public Safety - Local Government Emergency Response Loan Fund

licat.summary: The Legislature authorizes the State Division of Finance to transfer the following amounts between the following funds or accounts as indicated. Expenditures and outlays from the funds to which the money is transferred must be authorized by an appropriation.

lineitem.name: General Fund Restricted - Indigent Defense Resources Restricted Account
lineitem.lidesc: General Fund Restricted - Indigent Defense Resources Restricted Account

lineitem.name: General Fund Restricted - Victim Services Restricted Account
lineitem.lidesc: General Fund Restricted - Victim Services Restricted Account

lineitem.name: General Fund Restricted - Employment Incentive Restricted Account
lineitem.lidesc: General Fund Restricted - Employment Incentive Restricted Account

lineitem.name: General Fund Restricted - DNA Specimen Restricted Account
lineitem.lidesc: General Fund Restricted - DNA Specimen Restricted Account

lineitem.name: General Fund Restricted - Road Rage Awareness and Prevention Restricted Account
lineitem.lidesc: General Fund Restricted - Road Rage Awareness and Prevention Restricted Account

program.name: Road Rage Awareness and Prevention Restricted Account
program.display: Road Rage Awareness and Prevention Restricted Account

lineitem.name: General Fund Restricted - Emergency Medical Services System Account
lineitem.lidesc: General Fund Restricted - Emergency Medical Services System Account

licat.summary: The Legislature has reviewed proposed revenues, expenditures, fund balances, and changes in fund balances for the following fiduciary funds.

lineitem.name: Indigent Inmate Trust Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Governor's Office - Indigent Inmate Trust Fund

lineitem.name: Financial Crimes Trust Fund
lineitem.lidesc: Office of the Attorney General - Financial Crimes Trust Fund

sectionText.summary: The following sums of money are appropriated for the fiscal year beginning July 1, 2026, and ending June 30, 2027. These are additions to amounts previously appropriated for fiscal year 2027.

licat.summary: Under the terms and conditions of Title 63J, Chapter 1, Budgetary Procedures Act, the Legislature appropriates the following sums of money from the funds or accounts indicated for the use and support of the government of the state of Utah.

lineitem.name: Administration
lineitem.lidesc: Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Administration

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Children's Legal Defense
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Children's Legal Defense

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Court Security Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Court Security Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Court Trust Interest
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Court Trust Interest

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Dispute Resolution Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Dispute Resolution Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - DNA Specimen Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - DNA Specimen Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Rest. - Justice Court Tech., Security & Training
appsrc.display: From General Fund Rest. - Justice Court Tech., Security Training

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Nonjudicial Adjustment Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Nonjudicial Adjustment Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Online Court Assistance Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Online Court Assistance Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - State Court Complex Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - State Court Complex Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Tobacco Settlement Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Tobacco Settlement Account

lineitem.name: Contracts and Leases
lineitem.lidesc: Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Contracts and Leases

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - State Court Complex Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - State Court Complex Account

lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Judicial Council/State Court Administrator report on the following Contracts and Leases line item performance measure for FY 2027: Execute and Administer Required Contracts within the Contract Terms (Target = 100%).

lineitem.name: Grand Jury
lineitem.lidesc: Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Grand Jury
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Judicial Council/State Court Administrator report on the following Grand Jury line item performance measure for FY 2027: Administer Called Grand Juries (Target = 100%).

lineitem.name: Guardian ad Litem
lineitem.lidesc: Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Guardian ad Litem

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Victim Services Restricted Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Victim Services Restricted Account

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Children's Legal Defense
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Children's Legal Defense

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Children's Legal Defense
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Children's Legal Defense, One-time

lineitem.name: Jury and Witness Fees
lineitem.lidesc: Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Jury and Witness Fees
lineitem.appintent: In accordance with UCA 63J-1-903, the Legislature intends that the Judicial Council/State Court Administrator report on the following Jury and Witness Fees line item performance measure for FY 2027: Timely Pay All Jurors, Witnesses, and Interpreters (Target = 100%).

lineitem.name: Court Security
lineitem.lidesc: Judicial Council/State Court Administrator - Court Security

appsrc.name: General Fund Restricted - Court Security Account
appsrc.display: From General Fund Restricted - Court Security Account

subsection.amend: Except as provided in Subsection (2), this bill takes effect July 1, 2026.
The actions affecting Section 1 (Effective upon governor's approval) take effect:
except as provided in Subsection (2)(b), May 6, 2026; or
if approved by two-thirds of all members elected to each house:
without the governor's signature, the day following the constitutional time limit of Utah Constitution, Article VII, Section 8; or
in the case of a veto, the date of veto override.
